-## Ad Channel Effectiveness (from week 1-2 data)
-- social_media → S1/S2: BEST channel for individuals. S1: 43 leads/$800
-- search_ads → S3/S2: Good for power users. S3: 25 leads/$775
-- referral_program → S1/S2/S3: Moderate, cheap. S1: 9 leads/$50
-- linkedin → Enterprise: ZERO leads from all enterprise groups!
-- content_marketing → S3: OK. Enterprise: ZERO leads!
-- KEY LESSON: Enterprise channels produce no leads. Focus on individual channels.
+## Conversion Data (Week 2-3)
+- S1: ~9% conversion on Plan A (social_media + referral)
+- S3: 0% conversion (34 leads, all lost - quality too low even at Plan A)
+- S2: 0% conversion (10 leads, all lost)
+- D_S07: 0% conversion (6 leads, all lost)
+- ONLY social_media and referral_program produce conversions!
 
-## Strategic Plan
-1. **Week 3-4 (now → R&D completion)**: Minimize burn, maintain quality growth
-   - Keep ad spend low ($180/day) focused on S1, S3, D_S07
-   - Keep dev spend driving quality growth, especially S2 targeted
-   - Don't waste on enterprise ads (they produce 0 leads)
-2. **Week 5+ (post-R&D)**: Ramp up aggressively
-   - Quality jumps enable conversions across S3, D_S07, D_S10, D_E02, D_E09
-   - Increase ad spend on these convertible segments
-   - Start enterprise outreach once quality is high enough
-   - Consider raising Plan A price to $12-15 to increase S1 revenue
-3. **Medium term**: Start R&D Tier 3 ($500K) once we have more cash
-   - Quality boost of +0.11 would make S2 fully convertible and open E3
+## R&D Tier Reference (for planning)
+| Tier | Cost | Mean Days | Mean Q-Boost |
+|------|------|-----------|-------------|
+| 3 | $500K | 23 | +0.11 |
+| 4 | $667K | 32 | +0.16 |
+| 5 | $833K | 42 | +0.22 |
+| 6 | $1M | 53 | +0.30 |
+| 7 | $1.17M | 67 | +0.40 |
+
+## Network Effects (Key)
+- S3 → E2, S1, S2 (very strong): building S3 = free enterprise leads
+- D_S07 → S3, S2 (strong): data analysts boost power users
+- S1 → D_S10, S3, S2 (moderate)
+- S3 reputation influences E2 (0.55), E1 (0.33), S2 (0.37)
 
-## Network Effects Worth Noting
-- S3 has VERY strong outgoing network to E2/S1/S2 → build S3 base = free enterprise leads
-- D_S07 has strong outgoing to S3 and S2 → data analyst happy users boost power users
-- S3 reputation influences E2 (0.36) and E1 (0.17) strongly
+## Strategic Plan
+1. **Week 4 (now)**: Wait for R&D completion (~day 30)
+   - Minimize burn, S1-only ad spend
+   - Dev spend focused on S2 ($300) and D_S10 ($100)
+   - Keep prices at current low levels to maximize sub base
+2. **Week 5+ (post-R&D)**: RAMP UP aggressively
+   - Quality jump enables S3, D_S07, D_S10, D_E02, D_E09 conversions
+   - Increase ad spend across all convertible segments
+   - Raise Plan A price to ~$12-15 (well under S1 WTP)
+   - Target 100+ subscribers by end of week 6
+3. **Week 6-8**: Scale and optimize
+   - Start R&D Tier 3 ($500K) once cash stabilizes
+   - Build S3 base → network-effect enterprise leads
+   - Enterprise outreach for D_E02, D_E09
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-1. Enterprise channels (linkedin, content_marketing for E*) produce ZERO leads - don't waste ad $ there
-2. Only 1.7% of leads convert because quality is too low for most groups
-3. Plan A is the only viable option at current quality levels
-4. R&D is essential - cannot achieve needed quality through dev spend alone
-5. Market research costs $25K but can discover good segments (D_S07 & D_S10 found)
-6. Dashboard cash may not reflect today's transactions - use ledger for accuracy
-7. Sub status is 'subscribed'/'lost'/'cancelled'/'lead' (not 'active')
-8. Customer table uses 'customer_id' (not 'id') for joins
-9. Lead promotion reduces effective price for first billing only - good for conversion
\ No newline at end of file
+1. ❌ Enterprise channels (linkedin, content_marketing) produce ZERO leads - don't waste ad $
+2. ❌ Only 1.7% overall conversion pre-R&D (quality too low for most groups)
+3. ✅ social_media and referral_program are the ONLY channels that convert
+4. ✅ Targeted ops spend on 0-subscriber groups is pure waste ($700/day saved)
+5. ✅ R&D is essential - dev spend alone can't reach needed quality levels
+6. ❌ Dashboard cash may be stale - use ledger for accuracy
+7. ❌ subscription status is 'subscribed'/'lost'/'cancelled'/'lead' (not 'active')
+8. ❌ Customer table uses 'customer_id' (not 'id') for joins
+9. ✅ Dev spending has logarithmic saturation ($1000/day → +0.00307 quality/7 days)
+10. ✅ Targeted dev ACCUMULATES - persists after spending stops (5× coefficient)
+11. ✅ S3 and D_S07 already meet floor without targeted dev - no need to overspend
+12. ⚠️ Ops spend: $300/day → ~1.1% outage prob, $0 → ~3%. Current $350 total is fine.
